stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • On February 14, 1912, Arizona became the 48th state to join the US.
  • On January 3, 1959, Alaska became the 49th state to join the US.
  • A new 49-star US flag was unveiled later that year.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the order in which Arizona and Alaska became US states. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The 48th and 49th states to join the US did so in different decades.
Arizona, the 48th US state, became a state several decades before Alaska, the 49th state, did.
In 1959, a new 49-star US flag was unveiled.
The same year that Alaska became a US state—1959—a new US flag was unveiled.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the order in which Arizona and Alaska became US states, noting that Arizona became a US state several decades before Alaska did.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence indicates that the 48th and 49th states joined in different decades, it doesn't identify which state was the 48th and which was the 49th; as a result, it doesn't emphasize the order in which Arizona and Alaska became states. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence specifies when the 49-star US flag was unveiled; it doesn't emphasize the order in which Arizona and Alaska became US states. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence indicates that a new US flag was unveiled when Alaska became a US state; it doesn't emphasize the order in which Arizona and Alaska became states.
